evals.prompt: I already have ID Capture working in my .NET MAUI app (see IntegratedDataCaptureManager.cs, IntegratedMainPageViewModel.cs, IntegratedMainPage.xaml, IntegratedMainPage.xaml.cs). For compliance we now need to (1) reject expired IDs, (2) reject IDs that expire within the next 3 months, and (3) reject holders under 18. When any of these happens, show the user a clear message explaining why. Keep everything else working.
evals.expected_output: The skill adds RejectExpiredIds = true, RejectIdsExpiringIn = new Duration(0, 3, 0), and RejectHolderBelowAge = 18 to the existing IdCaptureSettings in the DataCaptureManager, and expands the existing OnIdRejected to surface distinct messages for RejectionReason.DocumentExpired, DocumentExpiresSoon, and HolderUnderage. The rest of the MAUI integration (camera, IdCapture.Create, DataCaptureView binding, HandlerChanged overlay, OnIdCaptured, accepted documents, scanner, ResumeAsync/SleepAsync, MainThread dispatch) is preserved.

assertions.text: RejectExpiredIds = true is added to the IdCaptureSettings
RejectIdsExpiringIn is set to a Duration constructed as new Duration(...) representing 3 months
RejectHolderBelowAge is set to 18
The existing OnIdRejected handler is expanded to surface distinct messages for RejectionReason.DocumentExpired, RejectionReason.DocumentExpiresSoon, and RejectionReason.HolderUnderage
No verifier classes are invented; rejection is configured purely via IdCaptureSettings flags
UI work in the rejection handler is dispatched via MainThread.BeginInvokeOnMainThread (or Application.Current.Dispatcher.DispatchAsync), not RunOnUiThread or DispatchQueue.MainQueue

The existing MAUI integration (camera setup, IdCapture.Create, DataCaptureView binding, IdCaptureOverlay in HandlerChanged, OnIdCaptured, AcceptedDocuments, FullDocumentScanner, ResumeAsync/SleepAsync lifecycle) is preserved

evals.prompt: I have ID Capture working in my .NET MAUI app (see IntegratedDataCaptureManager.cs, IntegratedMainPageViewModel.cs, IntegratedMainPage.xaml, IntegratedMainPage.xaml.cs). I'm scanning US driver's licenses and I need to detect forged AAMVA barcodes and reject them, and also flag licenses where the front and back data don't match. When a license is captured, I want to read the verification result so I can show whether the barcode looked authentic. How do I do this in a MAUI app?
evals.expected_output: The skill enables RejectForgedAamvaBarcodes = true and RejectInconsistentData = true on the IdCaptureSettings (NO standalone AamvaBarcodeVerifier / DataConsistencyVerifier class, which don't exist on .NET), and reads capturedId.VerificationResult in OnIdCaptured: AamvaBarcodeVerification.Status (AamvaBarcodeVerificationStatus.Authentic/LikelyForged/Forged) and DataConsistency.AllChecksPassed. It also notes that a confirmed forgery surfaces via OnIdRejected with RejectionReason.ForgedAamvaBarcode (and InconsistentData). The rest of the MAUI integration is preserved, and any UI work is dispatched via MainThread.BeginInvokeOnMainThread.

assertions.text: RejectForgedAamvaBarcodes = true is set on the IdCaptureSettings
RejectInconsistentData = true is set on the IdCaptureSettings
No standalone AamvaBarcodeVerifier or DataConsistencyVerifier class is instantiated or referenced (these do not exist on .NET; verification is settings-driven)
capturedId.VerificationResult is read in OnIdCaptured
The AAMVA outcome is read via VerificationResult.AamvaBarcodeVerification and its Status (AamvaBarcodeVerificationStatus.Authentic / LikelyForged / Forged)
The data-consistency outcome is read via VerificationResult.DataConsistency (e.g. AllChecksPassed)
It explains that hard failures surface via OnIdRejected with RejectionReason.ForgedAamvaBarcode (and/or InconsistentData)
Any UI work is dispatched via MainThread.BeginInvokeOnMainThread (or Application.Current.Dispatcher.DispatchAsync), not RunOnUiThread or DispatchQueue.MainQueue
The existing MAUI integration (camera, IdCapture.Create, DataCaptureView binding, HandlerChanged overlay, accepted documents, scanner, OnIdCaptured/OnIdRejected) is preserved

evals.prompt: In my .NET MAUI ID Capture app (see IntegratedDataCaptureManager.cs, IntegratedMainPageViewModel.cs, IntegratedMainPage.xaml, IntegratedMainPage.xaml.cs) I scan passports but for privacy reasons I must not retain the document number or any document images. How do I keep that regulated data out of the captured result in C#?
evals.expected_output: The skill sets IdCaptureSettings.AnonymizationMode to an IdAnonymizationMode value (FieldsAndImages, or FieldsOnly plus ImagesOnly as appropriate), and/or uses settings.AddAnonymizedField(document, IdFieldType.DocumentNumber) for the document-number field. It notes the result can be checked with capturedId.IsAnonymized(IdFieldType.DocumentNumber) and capturedId.AnonymizedFields. The rest of the MAUI integration is preserved.

assertions.text: IdCaptureSettings.AnonymizationMode is set to an IdAnonymizationMode value
An IdAnonymizationMode value that redacts images is used (IdAnonymizationMode.FieldsAndImages or IdAnonymizationMode.ImagesOnly)
The document number is anonymized, either via the AnonymizationMode covering fields or via settings.AddAnonymizedField with IdFieldType.DocumentNumber
No invented anonymization API is used; anonymization is configured on IdCaptureSettings (semantic)
The existing MAUI integration (camera, IdCapture.Create, DataCaptureView binding, HandlerChanged overlay, AcceptedDocuments, scanner, OnIdCaptured) is preserved

evals.prompt: In my .NET MAUI ID Capture app (see IntegratedDataCaptureManager.cs, IntegratedMainPageViewModel.cs, IntegratedMainPage.xaml, IntegratedMainPage.xaml.cs) I want to pull the document images out of the result so I can display the holder's face photo and a cropped picture of the document. How do I configure this and read the images in C#?
evals.expected_output: The skill enables the image types via settings.SetShouldPassImageTypeToResult(IdImageType.Face, true) and SetShouldPassImageTypeToResult(IdImageType.CroppedDocument, true), then reads capturedId.Images.Face and capturedId.Images.GetCroppedDocument(IdSide.Front) (and/or IdSide.Back) in OnIdCaptured. It notes the images are platform-typed (Android.Graphics.Bitmap? on Android, UIKit.UIImage? on iOS) and must be guarded with #if ANDROID / #if IOS. The rest of the MAUI integration is preserved.

assertions.text: settings.SetShouldPassImageTypeToResult is called with IdImageType.Face
settings.SetShouldPassImageTypeToResult is called with IdImageType.CroppedDocument
capturedId.Images.Face is read in OnIdCaptured
capturedId.Images.GetCroppedDocument is called with an IdSide value such as IdSide.Front
The images are described as platform-typed Android.Graphics.Bitmap on Android and UIKit.UIImage on iOS, guarded with #if ANDROID and #if IOS
No SetIncludeImage method is invented; image inclusion is controlled via SetShouldPassImageTypeToResult (semantic)
The existing MAUI integration (camera, IdCapture.Create, DataCaptureView binding, HandlerChanged overlay, AcceptedDocuments, OnIdCaptured) is preserved

evals.prompt: In my .NET MAUI ID Capture app (see IntegratedDataCaptureManager.cs, IntegratedMainPageViewModel.cs, IntegratedMainPage.xaml, IntegratedMainPage.xaml.cs) I only need to read the MRZ of passports — nothing else. How do I configure the scanner for just the MRZ and read the MRZ fields in C#?
evals.expected_output: The skill configures a SingleSideScanner with only the MRZ zone enabled (new SingleSideScanner(barcode: false, machineReadableZone: true, visualInspectionZone: false)) wrapped in IdCaptureScanner, and reads the MRZ result via capturedId.Mrz (e.g. CapturedMrz, DocumentCode). It reads name/DOB/nationality from the top-level capturedId, not from Mrz. The rest of the MAUI integration is preserved.

assertions.text: A SingleSideScanner is configured with machineReadableZone: true
The SingleSideScanner is wrapped in an IdCaptureScanner on IdCaptureSettings.Scanner
The MRZ result is read via capturedId.Mrz
An MrzResult field such as CapturedMrz is read off capturedId.Mrz
The existing MAUI integration (camera, IdCapture.Create, DataCaptureView binding, HandlerChanged overlay, OnIdCaptured) is preserved

evals.prompt: In my .NET MAUI ID Capture app (see IntegratedDataCaptureManager.cs, IntegratedMainPageViewModel.cs, IntegratedMainPage.xaml, IntegratedMainPage.xaml.cs) I need the printed front-of-card data — specifically the issuing authority and place of birth on ID cards. How do I configure the scanner for the visual inspection zone and read those fields in C#?
evals.expected_output: The skill configures a SingleSideScanner with only the VIZ zone enabled (new SingleSideScanner(barcode: false, machineReadableZone: false, visualInspectionZone: true)) wrapped in IdCaptureScanner, and reads capturedId.Viz for VIZ-specific fields such as IssuingAuthority and PlaceOfBirth. It notes that name/DOB/nationality/documentNumber are read from the top-level capturedId, not from Viz. The rest of the MAUI integration is preserved.

assertions.text: A SingleSideScanner is configured with visualInspectionZone: true
The VIZ result is read via capturedId.Viz
A VizResult field such as IssuingAuthority or PlaceOfBirth is read off capturedId.Viz
The existing MAUI integration (camera, IdCapture.Create, DataCaptureView binding, HandlerChanged overlay, OnIdCaptured) is preserved

evals.prompt: In my .NET MAUI ID Capture app (see IntegratedDataCaptureManager.cs, IntegratedMainPageViewModel.cs, IntegratedMainPage.xaml, IntegratedMainPage.xaml.cs) I want to reject voided documents (punched or cancelled IDs) and show the user a clear message asking them to present a valid document. How do I do this in C#?
evals.expected_output: The skill sets RejectVoidedIds = true on the IdCaptureSettings and handles RejectionReason.DocumentVoided in OnIdRejected with a user-facing message dispatched via MainThread.BeginInvokeOnMainThread. No verifier class is invented. The rest of the MAUI integration is preserved.

assertions.text: RejectVoidedIds = true is set on the IdCaptureSettings
RejectionReason.DocumentVoided is handled in OnIdRejected
A user-facing message for the voided case is dispatched via MainThread.BeginInvokeOnMainThread (or Application.Current.Dispatcher.DispatchAsync), not RunOnUiThread or DispatchQueue.MainQueue
The existing MAUI integration (camera, IdCapture.Create, DataCaptureView binding, HandlerChanged overlay, AcceptedDocuments, scanner, OnIdCaptured/OnIdRejected) is preserved

evals.prompt: In my .NET MAUI app I already have ID Capture working (see IntegratedDataCaptureManager.cs, IntegratedMainPageViewModel.cs, IntegratedMainPage.xaml, IntegratedMainPage.xaml.cs). I now also want to scan plain QR codes in the same app by adding a BarcodeCapture mode alongside IdCapture on the same DataCaptureContext. Can I run both modes at the same time?
evals.expected_output: The skill explains that IdCapture cannot run simultaneously with another mode such as BarcodeCapture on the same DataCaptureContext — mode exclusivity. It does NOT add a BarcodeCapture mode alongside IdCapture; instead it recommends a separate flow, and notes the back-of-license PDF417/AAMVA reader is already bundled into IdCapture so no BarcodeCapture is needed for that. The existing MAUI ID Capture integration is preserved.

assertions.text: It states that IdCapture cannot be used simultaneously with another mode such as BarcodeCapture on the same DataCaptureContext (semantic)
It does not add a BarcodeCapture mode alongside the existing IdCapture mode (semantic)
It recommends running IdCapture as the only mode and using a separate flow for plain barcode scanning (semantic)
The existing MAUI ID Capture integration (DataCaptureContext, IdCapture.Create, camera, DataCaptureView binding, OnIdCaptured) is preserved
